question.text: What is the ELM for deep out-of-the-money index options (strikes >10% OTM)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For index options that are deep OTM (strikes out of the money by more than 10% from previous day closing underlying price), the ELM shall be 3%. **The Trap:** Standard ELM for short index options is 2%. Deep OTM index options attract a higher 3% ELM. **Cross-Exam Context:** Deep OTM options carry unique tail risks that justify higher margins. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Deep OTM options may seem safe but can cause massive losses during extreme market moves.
